text: Code refactoring is the process of improving the internal structure of existing code without changing its external behavior. The goal is to make the code cleaner and easier to understand. For example, you might rename confusing variables, break a long function into smaller pieces, or remove duplicate sections. These changes do not add new features or fix bugs. Instead, they focus on readability and maintainability. Over time, messy code creates what developers call "technical debt." This debt makes future changes slower and riskier. Regular refactoring helps you pay off that debt, keeping your codebase healthy and your team more productive.
